steps.prompt: Every round thing is built from the same parts

graphic.name: wheel
graphic.caption: A cart wheel: every spoke runs from the center to the rim, and the bar straight across is the longest line of all. Bikes, clocks, pizzas — same parts every time.

steps.body:
A circle is more than one round line. Mathematicians give special names to the straight lines you can draw on it, and each one has its own job.
In this warm-up you'll meet five of them — radius, chord, diameter, secant, and tangent — and you'll keep using these words for the rest of the course. Each part gets its own colour so it's easy to spot.

steps.prompt: Tap each part of the circle
steps.doneNote: That's the core vocabulary — five lines you'll use all course.

targets.prompt: Tap the [radius](purple) — the line from the center O straight out to the rim.
targets.label: Radius
targets.hint: It starts at the middle dot O and stops at the edge — half of a diameter.

targets.prompt: Tap a [chord](green) — a straight line joining two points on the rim, like a guitar string across the hole.
targets.label: Chord
targets.hint: Both ends sit on the rim, but it skips the center.

targets.prompt: Tap the [diameter](orange) — the chord that runs straight through the center, rim to rim.
targets.label: Diameter
targets.hint: It's the longest chord — the one passing through the center O.

targets.prompt: Tap the [secant](blue) — a line that slices through the circle at two points and keeps going.
targets.label: Secant
targets.hint: Like a chord, but it carries on past both ends (note the arrows).

targets.prompt: Tap the [tangent](red) — a line that just grazes the rim at a single point, like a road touching a roundabout.
targets.label: Tangent
targets.hint: It touches the edge once and never goes inside.

steps.prompt: Sort each line by what it does
steps.subtitle: Tap a card, then tap its group. Tip: first check whether it reaches the middle dot O.
steps.doneNote: Sorted! The [radius](purple) and [diameter](orange) reach the center; the [chord](green) and [secant](blue) cross the rim twice; the [tangent](red) only grazes it once.

steps.prompt: Match each part to what it means
steps.subtitle: Tap a term, then tap its meaning — or the other way around.
steps.doneNote: Vocabulary locked in. Remember: the radius reaches halfway, the diameter all the way.

pairs.right: From the center out to the rim — reaches halfway across
Joins two points on the rim, like a guitar string across the hole
A chord through the center — reaches all the way across
A road that cuts through the circle at two points
A road that just touches the circle at one point

steps.prompt: A student described a circle. Find the wrong line.
steps.subtitle: One line gets a part's job wrong — tap it.

lines.text: A radius goes from the center O out to the rim.
A chord joins two points on the rim.
A tangent cuts across the circle at two points.
A secant cuts across and keeps going past both ends.

steps.explanation: A [tangent](red) only touches the circle at one point — it never goes inside. The line that cuts across at two points is the [secant](blue). Line 3 mixed the two up.
steps.hint: Which word means 'just touches at one point'?
steps.doneNote: Yes — that's a secant's job, not a tangent's.

steps.prompt: How long is the diameter compared with the radius?
steps.subtitle: The [radius](purple) reaches halfway; the [diameter](orange) reaches the whole way across.

options.label: The same length
options.feedback: The radius only reaches halfway — from the center to the rim.

options.label: Half as long
options.feedback: Other way around: the radius is the shorter one.

feedback.correct: Right — the diameter spans the whole circle, so diameter = 2 × radius.
feedback.incorrect: Compare how far each one reaches across the circle.
